author: @jonathancollister7483
text:
a long time ago I used to make wind spinners from wood which were similar-ish to this and did not need anything more than a saw and drill to produce. plus threaded bar and bearings.
I used wood which was cut into slats about an inch wide and 12 inches long and 3/8 thick. In the centre of the slat drill a hole to take a threaded bar. The slats were stacked on to the bar leaving enough room to put a washer and nuts either end and some bearings. The largest I made was 3 feet long and it needed a good attachment to a wall to hold it as it had a fair weight to it and spun at quite a rate. I had plans to put a generator on one to see what it would produce but never got around to it. Assembled in this manner produced a flat panel which could then be arranged by moving each slat up to a third ish of its width at the outside edge relative to the slat below it to produce a helix. Pretty much any wood can be used from ply to hardwood. I used short lengths of timber and ran them through a table saw cutting slices from the side of the timber 3/81/4 was about ideal ( from memory) to get a nice helix but that depended on the length of threaded bar I had. (Ply would have to be cut  on the face rather than the side). Also if you wanted to move the spinner you just moved the slats back so it returned to a flat panel.

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
This comes from a Veterinary Nurse,(Retired.) Nowadays, I Rescue/Rehab and Release Wildlife,,, Especially Birds,,,,To be Fair,, Cars,,Cats,,Pesticides,,Dogs,(Yes.)Humans with Airguns,,Herbicides,,Getting caught in netting,,Fishing tackle in a Wide Variety as a of Death-Traps,,Oiling from Spillage/Pollution from Ships,,Etc,,,Etc,,,
Those are the Main Causes of Injury/Sickness/ Killers of our Brithish and Migrating Birds,,

In Over 50Years of  helping to *Pick up The Pieces*~Only ONCE  Has it Anything to do with a WindTurbineS,,,(I Agree, even One is Too Many.) 
And, this was a WoodPigeon, who had Perched on a Horizontal Turbine, &, got a Helluva Suprise when a Gust of Wind Projected him, headfirst into a nearby Window,,,,Stunned, with a couple of broken toes,( he Survived! & Lives nearby.)


Humans are Really very Weird, about what is Really of *Concern to Wildlifes Welfare and Wellbeing. (*FIREWORKS- ATM)
Thank you Robert, Inspiring as Always.

author: @CarlGolden
text: With the black pipe. You mentioned leaving a section in the middle for stiffness. I wonder if each the top and bottom section were off by 60°. There for when one part were to lose wind pressure the opposite end would be there to pick up to increase the efficiency. I don't know much I was just wondering. I may be totally wrong. Just came across your channel and this is something that I have been interested in for some time to produce free energy.
